texto: The Church of Christ is constituted in two orders, the clergy and the people, the one having the care of the Church that all may be ruled for the salvation of souls; the other contains kings, princes, and nobles who have to carry on secular government that all things may lead to the peace and unity of the Church.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Thomas Becket sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “The Church of Christ is constituted in two orders, the clergy and the people, the one having the …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, unity, government, útil em momentos de government e people.

frase_original: The Church of Christ is constituted in two orders, the clergy and the people, the one having the care of the Church that all may be ruled for the salvation of souls; the other contains kings, princes, and nobles who have to carry on secular government that all things may lead to the peace and unity of the Church.

texto: Who shall resist Anti-Christ when he comes if we show such patience towards the vices and crimes of his precursors? By such leniency, we encourage kings to become tyrants and tempt them to withdraw every privilege and all jurisdiction from the Churches.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Thomas Becket que provoca enfrentar incertezas com ação. Ao questionar “Who shall resist Anti-Christ when he comes if we show such patience towards the vices and crimes …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre coragem, kings, privilege, com foco em privilege e show.

texto: Many are needed to plant and water what has been planted now that the faith has spread so far and there are so many people... No matter who plants or waters, God gives no harvest unless what is planted is the faith of Peter and unless he agrees to his teachings.
explicacao: Citação de Thomas Becket sobre temas ligados a fe. “Many are needed to plant and water what has been planted now that the faith has spread so far and…” articula fe, god, water em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como water e harvest.

texto: Remember the sufferings of Christ, the storms that were weathered... the crown that came from those sufferings which gave new radiance to the faith... All saints give testimony to the truth that without real effort, no one ever wins the crown.
explicacao: Citação de Thomas Becket sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Remember the sufferings of Christ, the storms that were weathered... the crown that came from tho…” articula reflexao, faith, effort em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e effort.
